meaning_en: A group of people sharing the same origin or characteristics
example: People of all races and religions live together here.
mnemonic: RACE — traCE; tracing your roots reveals your race.

meaning_en: Not happening often; unusual and valuable
example: A white tiger is a rare animal.
mnemonic: RARE — a HARE is RARE to spot in the wild.

meaning_en: The state of things as they actually exist
example: The reality is that learning takes a lot of time.
mnemonic: REALITY — REAL + ITY; the state of being real.

meaning_en: To refuse to accept or agree to something
example: Her application was rejected due to missing documents.
mnemonic: REJECT — RE + JECT; throw it back (ject = throw) = reject.

meaning_en: Consistently good; able to be trusted
example: She is a reliable worker who never misses deadlines.
mnemonic: RELIABLE — RELY + ABLE; you can rely on someone reliable.

meaning_en: A system of belief in a god or gods
example: India is home to people of many different religions.
mnemonic: RELIGION — RELI + GION; a REGION of beliefs = religion.

meaning_en: To stay in the same place or condition
example: The situation remains unclear despite investigations.
mnemonic: REMAIN — RE + MAIN; stay at the MAIN place and remain.

meaning_en: To cause someone to remember something
example: Remind me to call the doctor tomorrow morning.
mnemonic: REMIND — RE + MIND; bring something back to the MIND = remind.

meaning_en: Far away from where people live; small and unlikely
example: They live in a remote village with no internet access.
mnemonic: REMOTE — RE + MOTE; a mote (tiny speck) far away = remote.

meaning_en: To act or speak on behalf of someone
example: She was chosen to represent her school at the competition.
mnemonic: REPRESENT — RE + PRESENT; PRESENT yourself again for others.

meaning_en: To need something for a particular purpose
example: This job requires excellent communication skills.
mnemonic: REQUIRE — RE + QUIRE; things that you DESIRE and require.

meaning_en: Admiration for someone because of their qualities
example: Always treat your elders with respect.
mnemonic: RESPECT — RE + SPECT; look (spect) again and again at someone great = respect.

meaning_en: A duty to deal with or take care of something
example: It is your responsibility to complete the task on time.
mnemonic: RESPONSIBILITY — RESPOND + ABILITY; the ABILITY to RESPOND to duties.

meaning_en: The consequence or outcome of an action
example: Hard work always brings good results.
mnemonic: RESULT — RE + SULT; the SALT of your efforts = result.

meaning_en: To stop working, usually because of age
example: He retired at 60 after 35 years of service.
mnemonic: RETIRE — RE + TIRE; when you're too TIRED to work, you retire.

meaning_en: The possibility of something bad happening
example: There is a risk of flooding in this area during monsoon.
mnemonic: RISK — a DISC on a BRISK day — RISK taking is like spinning a disc.

meaning_en: Relating to love; creating feelings of love and excitement
example: They had a romantic dinner on their anniversary.
mnemonic: ROMANTIC — ROME + ANTIC; ancient ROME was the city of love — romantic.

meaning_en: Not smooth; difficult or unpleasant
example: The road was rough and full of potholes.
mnemonic: ROUGH — TOUGH; rough times are tough to handle.

meaning_en: Being protected from harm or danger
example: Always wear a seatbelt for your own safety.
mnemonic: SAFETY — SAFE + TY; full of safety; being safe.

meaning_en: Related to science or using scientific methods
example: The experiment was based on scientific evidence.
mnemonic: SCIENTIFIC — SCIENCE + IFIC; making science terrific.

meaning_en: The state of being safe from danger; protection
example: The bank has strong security systems in place.
mnemonic: SECURITY — SECURE + ITY; the state of being secure.

meaning_en: Showing good judgement; practical and reasonable
example: It is sensible to save money each month.
mnemonic: SENSIBLE — SENSE + IBLE; having good SENSE = sensible.

meaning_en: In a way that is bad or dangerous; without joking
example: Please take your studies seriously.
mnemonic: SERIOUSLY — SERIOUS + LY; in a serious manner.

meaning_en: Having a thin edge or point; intelligent; sudden
example: Be careful with that knife — it's very sharp.
mnemonic: SHARP — a HARP has sharp strings that cut through sound.

meaning_en: Nervous and uncomfortable around other people
example: He is shy and finds it hard to speak in public.
mnemonic: SHY — a FLY flies away when you reach out; shy creatures avoid contact.

meaning_en: Making no sound; saying nothing
example: The library must remain silent at all times.
mnemonic: SILENT — LISTEN (same letters); to truly listen, be silent.

meaning_en: Showing a lack of common sense; playfully foolish
example: Don't make silly mistakes in the exam.
mnemonic: SILLY — HILLY; a SILLY goat climbs HILLY roads for no reason.

meaning_en: Intelligent; stylish in appearance
example: She gave a very smart answer to the tricky question.
mnemonic: SMART — ARTS; a SMART person masters the ARTS.

meaning_en: People living together in an organised community
example: Education improves the quality of life in society.
mnemonic: SOCIETY — SOCIAL + ITY; the state of being social = society.

meaning_en: To find the answer to a problem
example: Can you solve this maths puzzle?
mnemonic: SOLVE — SALVE; a salve SOLVES the pain.

meaning_en: The non-physical part of a person; energy and determination
example: The team played with great spirit and energy.
mnemonic: SPIRIT — INSPIRE; a spirit INSPIRES you to keep going.

meaning_en: To open or extend over a wider area; to distribute
example: The news spread quickly through the town.
mnemonic: SPREAD — BREAD; you SPREAD butter on BREAD.

meaning_en: A large structure with tiers of seats for sporting events
example: The cricket stadium was packed with excited fans.
mnemonic: STADIUM — STAND + IUM; a place where people STAND and cheer.

meaning_en: A level of quality or achievement; a rule
example: The school maintains a high standard of education.
mnemonic: STANDARD — STAND + ARD; where you STAND on the quality scale.

meaning_en: Physical or mental power; a good quality
example: Her greatest strength is her ability to stay calm.
mnemonic: STRENGTH — STRENGTH comes from STRETCH; stretching builds strength.

meaning_en: Mental or physical tension caused by pressure
example: Exam stress affects many students.
mnemonic: STRESS — PRESS; stress is when life PRESSES hard on you.

meaning_en: A place where music, films, or art is created
example: She recorded her first song in a small home studio.
mnemonic: STUDIO — STUDY + O; a place to STUDY and create — a studio.

meaning_en: The achievement of something you wanted
example: Her success is the result of years of hard work.
mnemonic: SUCCESS — SUC + CESS; EXCESS effort leads to SUCCESS.

meaning_en: To experience pain, difficulty, or unhappiness
example: Many people suffer from stress and anxiety.
mnemonic: SUFFER — BUFFER; when the buffer breaks, you suffer.

meaning_en: Right or appropriate for a particular purpose
example: This film is not suitable for young children.
mnemonic: SUITABLE — SUIT + ABLE; a SUIT is ABLE to be worn for the right occasion.

meaning_en: To provide what is needed; the amount available
example: The government supplies free books to all students.
mnemonic: SUPPLY — APPLY; you APPLY to get the SUPPLY.

meaning_en: To continue to live after a difficult or dangerous situation
example: Few plants can survive in the desert heat.
mnemonic: SURVIVE — SUR + VIVE; VIVE means live in French; to keep living.

meaning_en: A natural skill or ability to do something well
example: She has a rare talent for playing the violin.
mnemonic: TALENT — tale + nt; a TALENT tells a great story.

meaning_en: Money paid to the government from earnings or purchases
example: All citizens are required to pay income tax.
mnemonic: TAX — a TAXI ride — you always pay a TAXI fare, just like tax.

meaning_en: A way of doing something that requires skill
example: She used a special breathing technique to stay calm.
mnemonic: TECHNIQUE — TECHNI + QUE; a unique (QUE) technical method.

meaning_en: The main subject or idea in a book, film, or discussion
example: The theme of the story is friendship and loyalty.
mnemonic: THEME — THE + ME; the heart (ME) of THE subject = theme.

meaning_en: Extremely small
example: The baby's fingers were tiny and delicate.
mnemonic: TINY — a MINI PONY is tiny.

meaning_en: A subject that is discussed, written, or studied
example: Today's topic for discussion is climate change.
mnemonic: TOPIC — TOP + IC; the TOP idea worth discussing = the topic.

meaning_en: The complete amount when everything is added together
example: The total cost of the trip was ₹15,000.
mnemonic: TOTAL — TOLL + AL; a total toll across all booths.

meaning_en: The buying and selling of goods; a job or skill
example: India has trade agreements with many countries.
mnemonic: TRADE — GRADE; traders GRADE their goods before selling.

meaning_en: A custom passed from one generation to the next
example: Wearing traditional clothes during festivals is a beautiful tradition.
mnemonic: TRADITION — TRAD + ITION; old TRADE routes carrying customs = tradition.

meaning_en: To change words from one language to another
example: Can you translate this letter from English to Hindi?
mnemonic: TRANSLATE — TRANS + LATE; carry words ACROSS (trans) to another language.

meaning_en: Medical care given to a patient; the way you deal with someone
example: She received treatment for her back pain.
mnemonic: TREATMENT — TREAT + MENT; the act of treating an illness or person.

meaning_en: A general direction in which things are changing
example: Social media shapes the latest trends in fashion.
mnemonic: TREND — BLEND; trends blend into popular culture.

meaning_en: The actual facts; what is real and correct
example: Always speak the truth, even when it is difficult.
mnemonic: TRUTH — RUTH; a RUTHLESS lie destroys TRUTH.

meaning_en: Not having the power, skill, or means to do something
example: She was unable to attend the meeting due to illness.
mnemonic: UNABLE — UN + ABLE; not ABLE to do something.

meaning_en: The state of not having a job
example: Unemployment is a major problem in many countries.
mnemonic: UNEMPLOYMENT — UN + EMPLOY + MENT; state of not being employed.

meaning_en: Not treating people equally or justly
example: It was unfair to punish the whole class for one person's mistake.
mnemonic: UNFAIR — UN + FAIR; the opposite of fair.

meaning_en: Not expected to happen; improbable
example: It is unlikely to rain this afternoon.
mnemonic: UNLIKELY — UN + LIKELY; not likely at all.

meaning_en: To give the latest information; to make something more modern
example: Please update your contact details on the form.
mnemonic: UPDATE — UP + DATE; bring the DATE up to the present = update.

meaning_en: Worth a lot of money; very useful
example: Time is the most valuable resource you have.
mnemonic: VALUABLE — VALUE + ABLE; having great VALUE = valuable.

meaning_en: A machine used for transporting people or goods
example: A bus is a vehicle that carries many passengers.
mnemonic: VEHICLE — VEHEMENT + ICLE; a vehicle moves with force.

meaning_en: A particular form of something that differs slightly from others
example: Have you seen the latest version of this app?
mnemonic: VERSION — VERSE + ION; each version is a new verse of the same story.

meaning_en: A person harmed by a crime, accident, or event
example: The victims of the flood received government aid.
mnemonic: VICTIM — VIC + TIM; poor TIM became a VICTIM of the storm.

meaning_en: Using physical force to hurt someone; very strong
example: Violent crime has increased in certain areas.
mnemonic: VIOLENT — VIOL + ENT; a violin bow that STRIKES hard = violent.

meaning_en: To offer to do something without being paid
example: She volunteers at the local hospital on weekends.
mnemonic: VOLUNTEER — VOLUNT + EER; a volunteer comes willingly (volunt = will).

meaning_en: To formally choose between options in an election or decision
example: Every citizen should vote in a democratic election.
mnemonic: VOTE — NOTE; VOTE by making a NOTE of your choice.

meaning_en: To tell someone about a possible danger
example: The doctor warned him to reduce his sugar intake.
mnemonic: WARN — HORN; a HORN warns you of an approaching vehicle.

meaning_en: To use more of something than is needed; unwanted material
example: Don't waste food — think of those who have nothing.
mnemonic: WASTE — HASTE; in HASTE, you WASTE everything.

meaning_en: An object used to injure or kill someone
example: The use of chemical weapons is banned internationally.
mnemonic: WEAPON — WEAP + ON; a weapon is turned ON against enemies.

meaning_en: Having a value equal to something; deserving something
example: This experience is worth every rupee spent.
mnemonic: WORTH — NORTH; heading NORTH is WORTH the journey.

meaning_en: To feel curious; a feeling of amazement
example: I often wonder what life is like in other countries.
mnemonic: WONDER — WANDER; you WONDER as you WANDER through new places.

meaning_en: Existing or happening throughout the world
example: The pandemic had a worldwide impact.
mnemonic: WORLDWIDE — WORLD + WIDE; spreading WIDE across the WORLD.

meaning_en: Young people collectively; the period of being young
example: The youth of today are more aware of global issues.
mnemonic: YOUTH — YOU + TH; YOU in your THirties remember your youth.

meaning_en: Eager to know or learn something
example: She was curious about how the machine worked.
mnemonic: CURIOUS — CURIOSITY killed the cat; being curious leads you to discoveries.

meaning_en: Able to change or adapt to new situations
example: We need a flexible schedule to adjust to changes.
mnemonic: FLEXIBLE — FLEX + IBLE; you FLEX your muscles to be flexible.

meaning_en: To make ready beforehand
example: Prepare your notes before the lecture begins.
mnemonic: PREPARE — PRE + PARE; pare (trim) things in advance = prepare.

meaning_en: Having knowledge or understanding of something
example: Are you aware of the changes to the timetable?
mnemonic: AWARE — A WARE; a wary person is always AWARE.

meaning_en: A set of questions asked to gather information from people
example: The company conducted a survey to understand customer needs.
mnemonic: SURVEY — SUR + VEY; look OVER (sur) the VIEW (vey) = survey.

meaning_en: A general direction in which things are developing
example: Working from home has become a major trend.
mnemonic: TREND — BLEND; a trend blends into mainstream culture.
